DISCOVERY, 610, TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS MODIFIED AS TO PLAINTIFF'S 1 AND 2. 1. MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS TO KAISER HOSPITAL & PMG/SAN FRANCISCO AND TO KAISER HOSPITAL & PMG/SF PSYCH DEPT OF PSYCHIATRY AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED. 2. NOTWITHSTANDING THE DENIAL OF RESPONDENT'S MOTION, THE COURT ORDERS THAT THE DISCLOSURE OF ANY AND ALL RESPONSIVE INFORMATION AND DOCUMENTS PRODUCED IN COMPLIANCE WITH THE SUBJECT SUBPOENAS SHALL BE LIMITED TO THE PARTIES, THEIR COUNSEL OF RECORD [AND ANY EMPLOYEE TO WHOM DISCLOSURE IS NECESSARY IN THE PROSECUTION OR DEFENSE OF THIS ACTION], AND/OR EXPERT CONSULTED OR RETAINED BY COUNSEL IN CONNECTION WITH THE SUBJECT CLAIM. IN THE EVENT THAT INFORMATION IS DISCLOSED TO ANY SUCH EMPLOYEE, EXPERT OR CONSULTANT, THOSE PARTIES SHALL BE INFORMED OF THESE RESTRICTIONS, AND PROVIDED WITH A COPY OF THIS ORDER. 3. AS TO CONTESTANT SCHLENKER'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS: ARGUED AND SUBMITTED. COMMISSIONER: PAUL SLAVIT. NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Respondent Forrest Canon To Quash Deposition Subpoenas To Kaiser Hospital & Pmg/Sf Psych Dept Of Psychiatry And Request For Sanctions; Declaration Of Stephanie Barber Hess In Support Of
Off Calendar Oct-05-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Stephanie Barber Hess, Esq., appearing by telephone, for respondent and moving party: Forrest Canon
Kathryn A. Schofield, Esq., for contestant and opposing party: Margaret Schlenker

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted as modified as to Plaintiff's 1 and 2. Respondent Forrest Canon's Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas To Kaiser Hospital & PMG/San Francisco And To Kaiser Hospital & PMG/SF Psych Dept Of Psychiatry And Request For Sanctions.
1. Motion to quash deposition subpoenas to Kaiser Hospital & PMG/San Francisco and to Kaiser Hospital & PMG/SF Psych Dept Of Psychiatry and request for sanctions denied; Evidence Code 1000 &1003 establish that the privilege does not exist with regard to the subject Will contest proceeding;
2. Notwithstanding the denial of respondent's motion, the court orders that the disclosure of any and all responsive information and documents produced in compliance with the subject subpoenas shall be limited to the parties, their counsel of record [and any employee to whom disclosure is necessary in the prosecution or defense of this action], and/or expert consulted or retained by counsel in connection with the subject claim. In the event that information is disclosed to any such employee, expert or consultant, those parties shall be informed of these restrictions, and provided with a copy of this order.
3. As to Contestant Schlenker's request for sanctions: argued and submitted.
Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-20-2009 Continued To Nov-03-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stephanie Barber Hess for Forrest Canon. Katherine Schofield for Margaret Schlenker.

This was a pre-granted matter. The Court stated that it did not have the opportunity to review the will contest filed by Ms. Schlenker. Ms. Schofield stated that discovery issues needed to be addressed. The matter was continued to November 3, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Jun-01-2009 Continued To Jun-29-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stephen Gizzi for Margaret Schlenker (Objector/Daughter). Adriana Dydell for Forrest Canon (Surviving Spouse).

Mr. Gizzi appeared to object.

The Court stated that the objections needed to be presented in writing and should be filed. The matter was continued to June 29, 2009.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Shannon.
